task.title: Report persisted discriminator on unsupported replay events
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/362 by ensuring unsupported-event replay diagnostics report the persisted event type and payload version rather than untrusted decoder-supplied context.
acceptance_criteria: A decoder returning divergent discriminator values still produces the persisted event type and payload version in ReplayError.
Existing unsupported-event behavior remains unchanged for decoders that echo stored context.
The public Event and error APIs remain unchanged.
Public event-log documentation identifies persisted discriminator values as authoritative.
The pinned local and required pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a decoder that deliberately returned fabricated unsupported-event context and asserted the replay boundary reports the stored row values.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test failed because replay returned decoder.fabricated at payload version 99 instead of the persisted account.opened at payload version 1.
attempts.patch: Added focused integration coverage in crates/vela-kernel/tests/event_log.rs without changing production behavior.

attempts.summary: Made the event log authoritative when mapping unsupported decode failures.
attempts.patch: Matched DecodeError::UnsupportedEvent without binding its context and constructed ReplayError::UnsupportedEvent from the event_type and payload_version read from SQLite.

attempts.summary: Preserved the public decoder API and documented ownership of diagnostic context.
attempts.diagnostic: Changing DecodeError would have introduced an unnecessary compatibility break; malformed payload text remains decoder-owned while stream position and persisted discriminator values remain log-owned.
attempts.patch: Kept Event and DecodeError unchanged and updated docs/event-log.md to state that stored discriminator values are authoritative.

attempts.summary: Ran focused and full verification, then obtained independent pre-commit and automated pull-request review.
attempts.diagnostic: The independent Codex review found no blocking findings. The GitHub Rust quality gate passed, and CodeRabbit generated no actionable comments with all five pre-merge checks passing.
attempts.patch: No corrective patch was required after review.

outcome.summary: Typed replay now treats the persisted row as the source of truth for unsupported event discriminators, producing trustworthy compatibility diagnostics without changing public APIs or storage format.

verification.command: /run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command codex review --uncommitted # result: no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate for pull request 363
CodeRabbit review for pull request 363 # result: no actionable comments
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0010-authoritative-replay-discriminator.json # result: valid development record
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 10 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Persistence boundaries should own diagnostics for facts read from storage rather than trusting duplicated context returned by a decoder.
An adversarial test double can expose authority confusion that well-behaved implementations hide by echoing their inputs.
Preserve a stable public API when a narrow boundary mapping can enforce the contract directly.
Separate ownership of error context explicitly: decoders own payload interpretation, while the log owns persisted discriminator and stream-position facts.
